newFixes: _Scene.tsx: pinned powerPreference to 'default' unconditionally (removed the gpuTier/!isWindows exception that still hit Firefox on dual-GPU macOS).
_Scene.tsx: added gl.failIfMajorPerformanceCaveat: false so SwiftShader / llvmpipe still produces a context (the WebGLErrorBoundary handles real failures).
_Scene.tsx: added onCreated callback that probes WEBGL_debug_renderer_info and console.warns when a software renderer is detected (NODE_ENV !== 'production' only).

SceneContainer.tsx: wrapped the dynamic import in .catch() so a chunk-load failure (CSP/ad-blocker/offline) returns a noop component instead of crashing the tree; the existing isMobile/low-tier guards already render SvgCoreFallback when the canvas would otherwise be attempted.
Atmosphere.tsx: removed the inline feTurbulence grain layer entirely (Firefox-mobile + Edge memory leak path); only the cheap repeating-linear-gradient scanlines remain.

globals.css: added a single body::after noise overlay gated behind @media (min-width: 1280px) and (hover: hover) so it only runs on desktop with a real mouse — no Firefox-mobile / coarse-pointer execution.
layout.tsx: added a <noscript> banner inside <body> instructing users to enable JS or contact ayan@bitpastel.com.
HeroWarrior.tsx: added WebkitClipPath alongside clipPath on the tech-chip and poster-fallback SVG-shape divs.
ProjectsSection.tsx: added WebkitClipPath alongside clipPath on the diamond proj-dot indicators.
result.summary: Phase 5 cross-browser audit: hardened WebGL bring-up (pinned powerPreference=default, failIfMajorPerformanceCaveat=false, software-renderer dev warning, dynamic-import .catch) and removed the inline feTurbulence grain (Firefox-mobile leak path) in favor of a single body::after overlay gated behind desktop-mouse @media. Added WebkitClipPath fallbacks to inline polygon clip-paths, plus a <noscript> banner. Verified prior phases (powerPreference default everywhere, material .clone() before mutation, no Environment presets, Lenis UA-detect for iOS Safari + Firefox + reduced-motion, IrisTransition opacity-only on isLowEnd with single box-shadow, @supports backdrop-filter fallback, single noise overlay). All :has() / 100dvh / unguarded matchMedia/navigator usages already absent or properly typeof-guarded. typecheck and build both pass.
verifiedFromEarlierPhases: components/three/_Scene.tsx — powerPreference was already gated to 'default' for non-(high+!Windows); now hard-pinned to 'default' across the board.
components/three/HeroWarrior.tsx — material clone via mat.clone() is in place inside cloneOne() before mutating envMapIntensity / emissiveIntensity (lines 112-129).
components/three/Lights.tsx — no <Environment preset> remains; only a comment referencing the removed warehouse preset. Hemisphere light replaces it.
components/three/HeroWarrior.tsx — no preset='city' remains; only a comment about the removed Environment preset.
components/providers/SmoothScrollProvider.tsx — Lenis disabled on iOS Safari (/iPad|iPhone|iPod/), Firefox (/Firefox/), and prefers-reduced-motion before any engine init.

components/ui/IrisTransition.tsx — returns null on caps.isLowEnd; uses opacity fade (no clipPath morph); single inset box-shadow on the mask wrapper, no dual drop-shadow filter on the mask itself (drop-shadow remains only on the small animated edge SVG path, which is the iris identity).
app/globals.css — @supports not (backdrop-filter) → solid fallback for .glass; -webkit-backdrop-filter set alongside backdrop-filter; coarse-pointer + reduced-motion media queries strip blur.
app/globals.css — duplicate noise overlay was already removed (.noise is a no-op shim).
